title: update_neighbor_coords: stores unvalidated coordinates
description:
File: python/src/lichen/routing/router.py:629-631

```python
def update_neighbor_coords(
    self, neighbor: IPv6Address, coords: tuple[float, float]
) -> None:
    """Update coords for a neighbor (from their announce)."""
    self.neighbor_coords[neighbor] = coords  # No validation!
```

While gpsr_forward() validates coords before USE (lines 794-799), update_neighbor_coords() stores them without any validation.

WHY IT'S A BUG: 
1. Invalid coords (NaN, inf, out-of-range, null island) are stored in neighbor_coords
2. gpsr_forward() must check every neighbor every time, instead of rejecting bad data at ingestion
3. Logging at line 796-797 happens on every GPSR lookup, not once at ingestion
4. Inconsistent with how dst_coords are handled (validated in gpsr_forward before any use)

IMPACT: Performance degradation from repeated validation; log spam from repeated warnings for same invalid neighbor; conceptual inconsistency in where validation occurs.

FIX: Validate in update_neighbor_coords and reject invalid coords:
```python
def update_neighbor_coords(self, neighbor: IPv6Address, coords: tuple[float, float]) -> bool:
    lat, lon = coords
    if not _validate_coords(lat, lon):  # extract validation to helper
        logger.warning("rejecting invalid coords for neighbor %s", neighbor)
        return False
    self.neighbor_coords[neighbor] = coords
    return True
```
